questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym for the given word. Revered
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option a. Revered: Deeply respected and admired. Venerated: Regarded with great respect; revered. Despised: Feel contempt or a deep repugnance for. Reviled: Criticized in an abusive or angrily insulting manner. Scorned: Felt or expressed contempt or derision for.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. The runner moved so g̲r̲a̲c̲e̲f̲u̲l̲ that everyone was mesmerized.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate option that can substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence is: gracefully "Gracefully" is the correct choice because it is the adverb form that appropriately modifies the verb "moved," describing how the action was performed. Thus, the sentence "The runner moved so gracefully that everyone was mesmerized" correctly conveys the intended meaning.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word. A servant leader, be it of a team, company, or nation, sets a good example, inspiring others with impeccable thoughts, words and deeds.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate antonym of the underlined word "impeccable" is: Shoddy Impeccable: Without fault or error; perfect. Shoddy: Badly made or done; of poor quality or craftsmanship. Immaculate: Perfectly clean, neat, or tidy. Meticulous: Showing great attention to detail; very careful and precise. Exemplary: Serving as a desirable model; representing the best of its kind.

questions.question: Parts of a sentence are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the parts in the correct order to form a meaningful sentence. P. Modern historians explain this development in a variety of ways: Magadha was a region where agriculture was especially productive. Q. Elephants, an important component of the army, were found in forests in the region. R. Between the sixth and the fourth centuries BCE, Magadha (in present-day Bihar) became the most powerful mahajanapada. S. Besides, iron mines (in present-day Jharkhand) were accessible and provided resources for tools and weapons. T. Also, the Ganga and its tributaries provided a means of cheap and convenient communication.
questions.explanation: The correct order to form a meaningful sentence is: RPSQT So, the sentence should read: " Between the sixth and the fourth centuries BCE, Magadha (in present-day Bihar) became the most powerful mahajanapada. Modern historians explain this development in a variety of ways: Magadha was a region where agriculture was especially productive. Besides, iron mines (in present-day Jharkhand) were accessible and provided resources for tools and weapons. Elephants, an important component of the army, were found in forests in the region. Also, the Ganga and its tributaries provided a means of cheap and convenient communication. "

questions.question: The following sentence has been divided into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. By the end of the week, they have already visited all the tourist attractions in the city.
questions.explanation: The segment that contains a grammatical error is: week, they have already The error is in the verb tense. "Have" is in the present perfect tense, but it should be in the past perfect tense to match the timeframe indicated by "By the end of the week." The correct form would be "had" instead of "have." Therefore, the corrected segment would be: “By the end of the week, they had already visited all the tourist attractions in the city.”

questions.question: Select the most appropriate idiom for the following sentence. He repeatedly brings up his failed relationship, refusing to discuss anything else and dwelling on past issues.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate idiom for the given sentence is: Harp on the same string Harp on the same string: To repeatedly talk about or emphasize the same topic, idea, or point, often in a way that becomes tiresome or annoying to others. To bite the dust: to suffer a defeat To cast pearls before swine: offer valuable things to people who do not appreciate them. Falling foul of: To come into conflict with or to violate a rule, regulation, or standard, resulting in trouble or criticism.

questions.question: Select the correct spelling from the given options to replace the underlined word in the following sentence. Great leaders such as M K Gandhi, Abraham Lincoln, Nelson Mandela and others like them, always endaevoured to serve others, which resulted in their leading humanity successfully towards life, liberty and happiness.
questions.explanation: The correct spelling to replace the underlined word is: Endeavoured Endeavoured: Made an effort to do or achieve something. Example: "Despite facing many challenges, she endeavoured to complete her project on time."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the following sentence. A servant-leader, who makes service a top priority, automatically embodies all the required qualities that we look for in a leader.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option b. The most appropriate synonym of the underlined word "embodies" in the sentence is: Personify Embodies: Represents or exemplifies a particular quality, idea, or characteristic in a tangible or visible form. Personify: To embody or represent a quality, concept, or thing in human form or character. Scatter: To throw in various random directions; to disperse. Abstract: Existing in thought or as an idea but not having a physical or concrete existence. Diverge: To move or extend in different directions from a common point; to separate.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. P. Several stories that originated in specific regions or circulated amongst certain people found their way into the epic. Q. The growth of the Mahabharata did not stop with the Sanskrit version. Over the centuries, versions of the epic were written in a variety of languages. R. At the same time, the central story of the epic was often retold in different ways. And episodes were depicted in sculpture and painting. S. through an ongoing process of dialogue between peoples, communities, and those who wrote the texts.
questions.explanation: The correct sequence is d: QSPR Q. The growth of the Mahabharata did not stop with the Sanskrit version. Over the centuries, versions of the epic were written in a variety of languages. This statement provides an overview of the expansion and evolution of the Mahabharata over time. S. through an ongoing process of dialogue between peoples, communities, and those who wrote the texts. This statement explains how the Mahabharata evolved through interactions and exchanges between different groups of people and authors. P. Several stories that originated in specific regions or circulated amongst certain people found their way into the epic. This statement elaborates on how various regional stories contributed to the content of the Mahabharata. R. At the same time, the central story of the epic was often retold in different ways. And episodes were depicted in sculpture and painting. This statement emphasizes the adaptability and versatility of the Mahabharata narrative, which was retold through various mediums and forms of art.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select ‘No substitution required’. She has been studying French for she was in high school.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment "for she was in high school" in the given sentence is: since The word "since" is used to indicate the starting point of an action that began in the past and continues into the present. In this sentence, "since" correctly indicates that the action of studying French started when she was in high school and has continued up to the present moment. Corrected Sentence: "She has been studying French since she was in high school."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. As a journalist, he embraced his role as a bird of nest, traveling to conflict zones and remote areas to report on stories from around the world.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate option to substitute the underlined segment "bird of nest" in the given sentence is: Bird of passage Bird of passage: Someone who frequently travels from place to place, often without settling down for long periods of time; a person who is transient or nomadic in nature.

questions.question: The following sentence has been divided into parts. One of them may contain an error. Select the part that contains the error from the given options. If you don’t find any error, mark ‘No error’ as your answer. Layer upon layers of historical evidence unraveled a complex narrative of the civilization’s rise and fall.
options: of the civilization’s rise and fall.
questions.explanation: The part that contains the error is: Layer upon layers of historical The error in the sentence is in the phrase "Layer upon layers of historical." According to the rule, when a preposition comes after a noun and then the same noun is repeated, the noun should be used in singular form. Therefore, "layer" should be singular in the phrase "Layer upon layer of historical evidence." Thus, the corrected sentence should be: "Layer upon layer of historical evidence unraveled a complex narrative of the civilization’s rise and fall."

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the following sentence in passive voice. The scientists conducted a series of intricate experiments to unravel the mysteries of quantum entanglement.
options: A series of intricate experiments were conducted by the scientists to unravel the mysteries of quantum entanglement.
A series of intricate experiments conducted by the scientists to unravel the mysteries of quantum entanglement.
A series of intricate experiments had conducted by the scientists to unravel the mysteries of quantum entanglement.
A series of intricate experiments had been conducted by the scientists to unravel the mysteries of quantum entanglement.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is a: “A series of intricate experiments were conducted by the scientists to unravel the mysteries of quantum entanglement.” Rule of simple past tense: Active voice ----- Passive voice Subject + V2 + object ----- Object + was/were + V3 + by + subject Passive voice: A series of intricate experiments were conducted by the scientists to unravel the mysteries of quantum entanglement.

questions.question: Select the correct word in the given options to fill in the blank. The company's new marketing strategy proved to be highly _______ in increasing sales and brand awareness.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is effective. In this context, the sentence is discussing the impact of the company's marketing strategy on sales and brand awareness, so the appropriate word to fill in the blank is "Effective," meaning successful or producing the desired result. Affective, refers to something related to emotions or feelings, which doesn't make sense in the context of the sentence.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. a speech or piece of writing that praises someone or something highly.
questions.explanation: The one-word substitute for "a speech or piece of writing that praises someone or something highly" is d: Encomium Encomium: A speech or piece of writing that praises someone or something highly; a tribute or eulogy. Reticent: Inclined to be silent or uncommunicative in speech; reserved. Garrulous: Excessively talkative, especially on trivial matters. Alacrity: Promptness in response; eagerness; brisk and cheerful readiness.

questions.question: Choose the option that correctly converts the following direct speech into indirect speech. Your father said to me, “I should not have given my brother such a long speech.”
options: Your father told me that he should not had given his brother such a long speech.
Your father said that he could not have given his brother such a long speech.
Your father told me that he should not have given his brother such a long speech.
Your father said that he should not had given his brother such a long speech.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is c: “Your father told me that he should not have given his brother such a long speech.” When converting this direct speech into indirect speech, we need to change the pronouns and verb tenses to make it grammatically correct within the context of the reported speech. Here's the process: "Said to me" becomes "told me." "I" becomes "he" (since the subject of the reported speech, "your father," is talking about himself). "Should not have" remains the same as "should not have" as should is a modal and it takes base form of verb after it. "Given" remains the same. "My brother" becomes "his brother" (since the subject of the reported speech, "your father," is talking about his own brother). "Such a long speech" remains the same. So, the correct conversion into indirect speech is: "Your father told me that he should not have given his brother such a long speech."

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. A group of singers in a church.
questions.explanation: The one-word substitute for "A group of singers in a church" is a: Choir Choir: A group of singers, especially in a church or cathedral, typically singing together as part of a religious service or musical performance. Band: A group of musicians who perform together, typically playing instruments such as guitars, drums, and keyboards. Troop: A group of people, especially soldiers or scouts, typically organized for a specific purpose or activity. Sonnet: A poem of fourteen lines using any of a number of formal rhyme schemes, in English typically having ten syllables per line.

questions.question: In the following sentence, four words are underlined, out of which one word is incorrectly spelt. Identify the INCORRECTLY spelt word. In Delhi, the scorching temperatures have led to a similar emergency, reigeniting an old dispute over water allocation to the capital.
questions.explanation: The incorrectly spelled word in the given sentence is: Option B) Reigeniting Reigniting: Starting or causing something to start burning or glowing again, or figuratively, renewing or reviving something, such as a discussion or conflict. Temperatures: The degree or intensity of heat present in a substance or object, typically measured in degrees Fahrenheit or Celsius. Dispute: A disagreement or argument between two or more parties, often concerning a matter of importance. Allocation: The action or process of distributing resources, funds, or tasks for a specific purpose or among various individuals or groups.

questions.question: Select the word segment that substitutes (replaces) the bracketed word segment correctly and completes the sentence meaningfully. We (doubted so) the weather would improve for the picnic.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Doubted if. If you really doubt that something is true (suspect that it's false), use “doubt that”: “I doubt that Fred has really lost 25 pounds.” If you want to express uncertainty, use “whether”: “I doubt whether we'll see the comet if the clouds don't clear soon.” “Doubt if” can be substituted for “doubt whether,”. So, the corrected sentence is: "We doubted if the weather would improve for the picnic."

questions.question: Select the correct word in the given options to fill in the blank. The police officer __________ the suspect's statement before making an arrest.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is considered. The past tense "before making" indicates that the action of considering the suspect's statement happened in the past, so the correct form of the verb is "considered."

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Good governance is not only about keeping the routine going but also about anticipating (1) _________ and smoking out fault lines, preventing the loss of human life, at the very least. Over the last couple of days in Kallakurichi, in north Tamil Nadu, 39 people (2) ________ after consuming hooch laced with methanol. With others admitted in hospital in a critical condition, the toll might go up even further, rendering this one of the most devastating hooch (3) _________ in recent times. It is not as if the signs were not obvious, particularly after the hooch tragedy which claimed several lives in 2023 in Marakkanam and Madhuranthakam, also in north Tamil Nadu, where it was clear that industrial methanol (4) _________ diverted to the illicit brewing cottage industry. The relatives of those dead have spoken about the easy availability of cheap hooch in the region regularly, and that the cost of the local moonshine is (5) __________ than the price of alcohol in the Tamil Nadu State Marketing Corporation Limited (TASMAC) shops. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 1.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “obstacles” Among the options given: • obstacles: This fits well because it suggests potential problems or challenges that need to be addressed, which aligns with the idea of anticipating and preventing issues. • functions: This doesn't fit the context as well, as it suggests roles or duties rather than potential problems. • drinking: This doesn't fit the context of governance and anticipating problems. • treatments: This also doesn't fit the context, as it suggests medical interventions rather than governance issues. Therefore, the most appropriate word to fill in the blank is "obstacles."

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Good governance is not only about keeping the routine going but also about anticipating (1) _________ and smoking out fault lines, preventing the loss of human life, at the very least. Over the last couple of days in Kallakurichi, in north Tamil Nadu, 39 people (2) ________ after consuming hooch laced with methanol. With others admitted in hospital in a critical condition, the toll might go up even further, rendering this one of the most devastating hooch (3) _________ in recent times. It is not as if the signs were not obvious, particularly after the hooch tragedy which claimed several lives in 2023 in Marakkanam and Madhuranthakam, also in north Tamil Nadu, where it was clear that industrial methanol (4) _________ diverted to the illicit brewing cottage industry. The relatives of those dead have spoken about the easy availability of cheap hooch in the region regularly, and that the cost of the local moonshine is (5) __________ than the price of alcohol in the Tamil Nadu State Marketing Corporation Limited (TASMAC) shops. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 2.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is ‘have died’ The options are: • has died: Incorrect, because "has" is singular, and we need a plural form to match "39 people." • are died: Incorrect, as "are died" is not a grammatically correct construction. • have died: Correct, as "have" is the plural form needed to agree with "39 people." • was died: Incorrect, as "was" is singular and "was died" is not a grammatically correct construction. Therefore, the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 2 is "have died."

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Good governance is not only about keeping the routine going but also about anticipating (1) _________ and smoking out fault lines, preventing the loss of human life, at the very least. Over the last couple of days in Kallakurichi, in north Tamil Nadu, 39 people (2) ________ after consuming hooch laced with methanol. With others admitted in hospital in a critical condition, the toll might go up even further, rendering this one of the most devastating hooch (3) _________ in recent times. It is not as if the signs were not obvious, particularly after the hooch tragedy which claimed several lives in 2023 in Marakkanam and Madhuranthakam, also in north Tamil Nadu, where it was clear that industrial methanol (4) _________ diverted to the illicit brewing cottage industry. The relatives of those dead have spoken about the easy availability of cheap hooch in the region regularly, and that the cost of the local moonshine is (5) __________ than the price of alcohol in the Tamil Nadu State Marketing Corporation Limited (TASMAC) shops. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 3.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Tragedies. The sentence is discussing a tragic event caused by consuming hooch laced with methanol, which resulted in loss of life. We need a word that appropriately describes such a tragic event. • Tragedy: A serious and sad event, especially one involving death or suffering. It can be used to describe a singular event. • Tragedies: The plural form of tragedy, indicating multiple serious events. • Brutality: Refers to extreme cruelty or violence, not typically used to describe events like this. • Tactfully: Refers to handling something in a sensitive or diplomatic manner, not suitable in this context. Given the context of describing a devastating event involving loss of life, "Tragedy" or "Tragedies" are the appropriate choices. However, considering the severity and the plural nature of the consequences mentioned (multiple deaths and potential critical conditions), "Tragedies" is more fitting. Therefore, the correct answer to fill in blank number 3 is: Tragedies

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Good governance is not only about keeping the routine going but also about anticipating (1) _________ and smoking out fault lines, preventing the loss of human life, at the very least. Over the last couple of days in Kallakurichi, in north Tamil Nadu, 39 people (2) ________ after consuming hooch laced with methanol. With others admitted in hospital in a critical condition, the toll might go up even further, rendering this one of the most devastating hooch (3) _________ in recent times. It is not as if the signs were not obvious, particularly after the hooch tragedy which claimed several lives in 2023 in Marakkanam and Madhuranthakam, also in north Tamil Nadu, where it was clear that industrial methanol (4) _________ diverted to the illicit brewing cottage industry. The relatives of those dead have spoken about the easy availability of cheap hooch in the region regularly, and that the cost of the local moonshine is (5) __________ than the price of alcohol in the Tamil Nadu State Marketing Corporation Limited (TASMAC) shops. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 4.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is ‘was being’ • was being: This is passive voice in the past continuous tense, indicating that something was happening at a specific point in the past. • were being: This is passive voice in the past continuous tense, used for plural subjects. • is being: This is present continuous tense, which does not fit the past context. • are being: This is present continuous tense, which also does not fit the past context. Given that the sentence talks about a specific past event and refers to the diversion of industrial methanol, the correct choice is the past continuous tense for a singular subject: was being

questions.question: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. Good governance is not only about keeping the routine going but also about anticipating (1) _________ and smoking out fault lines, preventing the loss of human life, at the very least. Over the last couple of days in Kallakurichi, in north Tamil Nadu, 39 people (2) ________ after consuming hooch laced with methanol. With others admitted in hospital in a critical condition, the toll might go up even further, rendering this one of the most devastating hooch (3) _________ in recent times. It is not as if the signs were not obvious, particularly after the hooch tragedy which claimed several lives in 2023 in Marakkanam and Madhuranthakam, also in north Tamil Nadu, where it was clear that industrial methanol (4) _________ diverted to the illicit brewing cottage industry. The relatives of those dead have spoken about the easy availability of cheap hooch in the region regularly, and that the cost of the local moonshine is (5) __________ than the price of alcohol in the Tamil Nadu State Marketing Corporation Limited (TASMAC) shops. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 5.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is ‘much lower.’ We are comparing the cost of local moonshine to the price of alcohol in TASMAC shops. We need to use a comparative form that correctly fits the sentence. • much lowest: Incorrect because "lowest" is a superlative form and "much" is not needed in this context. • most lowest: Incorrect for the same reason as above. • more lower: Incorrect because "lower" is already in the comparative form, so "more" is unnecessary. • much lower: This is the correct comparative form to use, indicating that the cost of local moonshine is significantly less than the price of alcohol in TASMAC shops. Therefore, the correct answer to fill in blank number 5 is: much lower
